steps.title: Identify y-intercept and slope from the graph
steps.content: The line on the graph passes through the y-axis at (0, -5), so the y-intercept is -5. The slope (rise over run) between (0, -5) and (1, -3) is $\frac{-3 - (-5)}{1 - 0} = \frac{2}{1} = 2$. Thus, the equation is $y = 2x - 5$.

steps.title: Test x-values in each choice
steps.content:
Plug $x = 0, 1, 2$ into $y = 2x - 5$:
- $x=0$: $y = -5$
- $x=1$: $y = 2(1) - 5 = -3$
- $x=2$: $y = 2(2) - 5 = -1$.
Only choice D matches all three values. D is correct.
